transcript: Hi my name is Kim Sterling and I am the Rosarian here at Rogers Gardens and today we're going to talk about organic rose care and with your organic gardening practices you can one help build your soil to attract beneficial insects and three help keep the planet green okay first we're going to with fertilizer. You want to find a really good organic fertilizer and you want to fertilize your roses every six to eight weeks. My favorite is Dr. Earth rose and flower food. The next thing I use is Epsom salts. I use Epsom salts to help promote some new growth on the plant to help for bigger blooms and overall health of the plant. I use half a cup for each rose plant. You sprinkle it around the base and water it in thoroughly. Okay, after you have your fertilizer and your ebbs and salts down, you're gonna wanna put down a layer of mulch. My favorite compost mulch is Booze Blend by Malibu Compost. You wanna make it about an inch thick and about one to two feet out from the base of the rose. Next we have insects. Usually you only have two insects to deal with with roses. One would be aphids and a good insecticidal spray will work on those. The other would be the sawfly larva which is a little green worm that gets on the underside of your leaves and will lace them out. You can use the ready to use BT spray and that will take care of those. Next we're going We'll be right back. green worm that gets on the underside of your leaves and will lace them out. You can use the ready-to-use BT spray and that will take care of those. Next we're going to move on to diseases. The prevention of the diseases will help with your thorough watering, no overhead watering, the cleanup of keeping the fallen leaves off the soil, and your mulch should help with the diseases. If you do get diseases you can use for powdery mildew the safer three-in-one spray. Also you can use liquid cop which is a concentrate and you mix yourself. This will work also on rust and black spot which is a common disease that you usually get towards the end of the summer. It was my pleasure to share my organic gardening practices with you. Hopefully you'll get out there and use these and have happy, healthy, and organic roses.
